reasoning: The informal statement asserts that every finite group of even order contains an element of order 2. The Lean signature takes a type G with Group and Fintype instances, assumes Even (card G), and concludes ∃ x : G, orderOf x = 2. The Fintype assumption is necessary to make sense of "even order" (card G) and matches the implicit finiteness in the informal statement. The conclusion ∃ x, orderOf x = 2 exactly captures "contains an element of order 2". The universal quantification over G is expressed by the type-class-polymorphic theorem signature. This is a standard and faithful encoding.
